task_completion.description: The target texture image is opened and visible in the image editor workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main application window (e.g., GIMP) is open, displaying a texture image (either a real photo or a checkerboard pattern) on the canvas.

task_completion.description: The Offset tool dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Offset' (typically accessed via Layer -> Transform -> Offset) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Non-zero offset values are entered for the X and/or Y axes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Offset dialog, the input fields for X and/or Y offset contain non-zero values (indicating a noticeable shift, ideally >=10% of the image dimensions).

task_completion.description: The 'Wrap around' edge behavior is selected in the Offset dialog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Offset dialog, under 'Edge Behavior' or similar settings, the 'Wrap around' option (or radio button) is explicitly selected.

task_completion.description: The offset transformation is applied to the image, demonstrating a seamless wrap-around effect.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final canvas shows the image shifted from its original position. The space left by the shift is filled with the content that was pushed off the opposite edge, leaving no transparent gaps or solid-color borders.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the image editor's menus and dialogs (e.g., GIMP), rather than a terminal window executing ImageMagick or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The wrap-around effect was achieved using the built-in tool, not by manually copying, pasting, and aligning image segments.
integrity.visual_evidence: The layers panel should show a single modified layer, without multiple duplicated layers, active selection marquees, or manual copy-paste operations used to fake the wrap-around.

integrity.description: The agent modified the provided image rather than downloading or importing a pre-shifted solution.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the original image being manipulated through the Offset dialog, rather than a new file being opened at the end.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP's 'Layer -> Transform -> Offset' feature. A correct execution must have the 'Wrap around' option selected in the Offset dialog. This ensures that when the image is shifted by X/Y pixels, the pixels pushed off one edge reappear on the opposite edge. The final image must contain all original pixels (>=90% pixel match in histogram/content) just shifted, with a noticeable displacement (>=10%), and absolutely no empty, transparent, or background-colored gaps at the edges.
